rag_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in valid AADL component type or implementation scopes based on the scope of the referenced ports and bindings.
Assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports should be placed in component type scopes, while implementation-level bindings go in implementation scopes.
generic_rules: AGREE annex blocks are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` and contain assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations.
Component type annexes are the standard location for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations.
Component implementation annexes are used for implementation-level bindings such as assignments when valid in that scope.
The authoritative source for component names, ports, directions, data types, subcomponents, and connections is the input AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE expressions must use valid AADL data types and variable names in scope; avoid using AADL property names directly as AGREE variables.
Boolean expressions should only be used for AGREE bool ports; avoid converting Boolean domain values into integers.
anti_patterns: Do not place component type contracts in implementation scopes unless they involve subcomponent port bindings.
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names are not AGREE variables
Assumed type vs actual type mismatch
